    • WAVE 1 — All Parallel
      • Task 1: WT-1 — Ship v0.5.0 Release Prep
      • Task 2: WT-2 — Phase 2: brain.scope() API + Cross-Domain Meta-Rules
      • Task 3: WT-3 — Adapter Verification
      • Task 4: WT-4 — Phase 7 Integration Test Gaps
      • Task 5: WT-5 — Ablation Baseline Experiment (budget ~$10)
      • Task 6: MiroFish — 3 Meta-Rule Sims (budget ~$3.50)
      • Task 7: BG-1 — Open Source Scenario Research
      • Task 8: MAIN — Supabase Migration
    • WAVE 2 — After MiroFish Completes
      • Task 9: Synthesize MiroFish + Optimize Weights
    • WAVE 3 — After Wave 2
      • Task 10: Ablation v2 — Improved System (budget ~$10)

    S101 Master Execution Plan¶

    For agentic workers: REQUIRED SUB-SKILL: Use superpowers:subagent-driven-development (recommended) or superpowers:executing-plans to implement this plan task-by-task. Steps use checkbox (- [ ]) syntax for tracking.

    Goal: Execute 9 parallel workstreams — 4 code worktrees, ablation baseline, 3 MiroFish sims, open source research — then run sequential Wave 2 (formula fix + weight tuning) and Wave 3 (ablation v2).

    Architecture: Master orchestrator dispatches independent sub-agents to worktrees and background scripts. Wave 1 is fully parallel. Wave 2 depends on MiroFish results. Wave 3 depends on Wave 2.

    Tech Stack: Python 3.12, pytest, DeepSeek V3 (generation), Claude Sonnet (judge, fallback DeepSeek-Reasoner), uv build, git worktrees

    Prerequisites: Both DEEPSEEK_API_KEY and ANTHROPIC_API_KEY must be set in the environment before execution.

    WAVE 1 — All Parallel¶

    Task 1: WT-1 — Ship v0.5.0 Release Prep¶

    Files: - Modify: src/gradata/__init__.py:21 - Modify: pyproject.toml:7 - Modify: CHANGELOG.md

    • Step 1: Create branch release/v0.5.0
    • Step 2: In src/gradata/__init__.py line 21, change __version__ = "0.2.0" to __version__ = "0.5.0"
    • Step 3: In pyproject.toml line 7, change version = "0.4.0" to version = "0.5.0"
    • Step 4: Add CHANGELOG.md v0.5.0 entry (self-healing PR#21, cloud backend PR#22, wiki injection, notifications, hooks PR#20, 210 ruff fixes, CI fixes)
    • Step 5: Verify: python -c "import gradata; assert gradata.__version__ == '0.5.0'"
    • Step 6: Security scan: grep -rn 'AKIA[A-Z0-9]' src/ --include='*.py' — expect no output
    • Step 7: Build: uv build — expect wheel + sdist
    • Step 8: Verify: ls dist/ | grep 0.5.0
    • Step 9: Clean: rm -rf dist/
    • Step 10: Commit: release: bump version to v0.5.0
    • Step 11: Tag: git tag -a v0.5.0 -m "v0.5.0" (do NOT push)

    Task 2: WT-2 — Phase 2: brain.scope() API + Cross-Domain Meta-Rules¶

    Files: - Modify: src/gradata/brain.py (add scope() after apply_brain_rules ~line 474) - Modify: src/gradata/enhancements/meta_rules.py (add detect_cross_domain_candidates) - Modify: src/gradata/enhancements/self_healing.py (add suggest_scope_narrowing) - Create: tests/test_scoped_brain.py

    • Step 1: Create branch feat/scoped-brains-api
    • Step 2: Write failing test — TestBrainScope with 4 test methods testing brain.scope(domain, task_type) returns str
    • Step 3: Run: pytest tests/test_scoped_brain.py -v -x — expect FAIL (no scope attr)
    • Step 4: Implement brain.scope(domain, task_type, agent_type, max_rules) — delegates to apply_brain_rules with context dict
    • Step 5: Run: pytest tests/test_scoped_brain.py::TestBrainScope -v — expect PASS
    • Step 6: Write failing test — TestCrossDomainPromotion testing detect_cross_domain_candidates(rules, min_domains=3)
    • Step 7: Run test — expect FAIL (ImportError)
    • Step 8: Implement detect_cross_domain_candidates in meta_rules.py — groups rules by normalized description, returns those appearing in 3+ domains
    • Step 9: Run test — expect PASS
    • Step 10: Write failing test — TestScopeRefinement testing suggest_scope_narrowing(rule_scope, misfire_context)
    • Step 11: Run test — expect FAIL (ImportError)
    • Step 12: Implement suggest_scope_narrowing in self_healing.py — narrows wildcard scope fields when rule fires in wrong context
    • Step 13: Run: pytest tests/test_scoped_brain.py -v — expect all PASS
    • Step 14: Run: pytest tests/ -x -q --tb=short — expect 1819+ passed
    • Step 15: Commit: feat: brain.scope() + cross-domain detection + scope narrowing

    Task 3: WT-3 — Adapter Verification¶

    Files: - Possibly modify: src/gradata/integrations/*.py - Test: tests/test_integrations.py

    • Step 1: Create branch fix/adapter-verification
    • Step 2: Run: pytest tests/test_integrations.py -v --tb=short — record failures
    • Step 3: Fix any broken imports/signatures in adapter files
    • Step 4: Smoke-test core API: correct(), apply_brain_rules(), prove() all return expected types
    • Step 5: Run: pytest tests/test_integrations.py -v — expect all PASS
    • Step 6: Run: pytest tests/ -x -q --tb=short — expect 1819+ passed
    • Step 7: Commit if fixes needed: fix: verify adapters against current Brain API

    Task 4: WT-4 — Phase 7 Integration Test Gaps¶

    Files: - Create: tests/test_atomic_writes.py - Create: tests/test_failure_modes.py - Create: tests/test_cascading_corrections.py

    • Step 1: Create branch test/integration-gaps
    • Step 2: Write test_atomic_writes.py — rapid sequential corrections, DB survives, lessons not truncated
    • Step 3: Write test_failure_modes.py — empty brain graceful degradation (apply_rules, prove, manifest, search all work)
    • Step 4: Write test_cascading_corrections.py — correction of correction, contradictory corrections, rapid 20x corrections all persisted
    • Step 5: Run: pytest tests/test_atomic_writes.py tests/test_failure_modes.py tests/test_cascading_corrections.py -v — expect all PASS
    • Step 6: Run: pytest tests/ -x -q --tb=short — expect 1819+ passed
    • Step 7: Commit: test: integration tests for atomic writes, failure modes, cascading corrections

    Task 5: WT-5 — Ablation Baseline Experiment (budget ~$10)¶

    Files: - Create: C:/Users/olive/SpritesWork/brain/scripts/ablation_experiment.py

    Script design: - Generation: DeepSeek V3 (deepseek-chat), temp 0.7, max 1024 tokens - Judging: Claude Sonnet (claude-sonnet-4-20250514) for blind pairwise scoring. Fallback to deepseek-reasoner if Anthropic fails. - 200 tasks across 5 domains (email 50, code 40, planning 40, sales 40, docs 30) - Task source: mine events.jsonl for real correction contexts, supplement synthetic - 3 conditions: bare LLM (A), static CLAUDE.md config (B), graduated brain rules (C) - 5 runs per condition per task = 3,000 generations - Pairwise judging: A-vs-B, A-vs-C, B-vs-C = 3,000 judge calls (randomized order) - 5 scoring dimensions: accuracy, voice, structure, conciseness, judgment (1-10) - Static config: curated 20 rules representing what Oliver would write manually - 10 concurrent requests, 3 retries with exponential backoff - Output: baseline_report.json + baseline_summary.md

    • Step 1: Read benchmark_before_after.py for reusable patterns
    • Step 2: Create ablation_experiment.py implementing all above
    • Step 3: Dry-run: python scripts/ablation_experiment.py --dry-run
    • Step 4: Run: python scripts/ablation_experiment.py --budget 10 (~20 min)
    • Step 5: Verify output in stress_test_results/ablation/

    Task 6: MiroFish — 3 Meta-Rule Sims (budget ~$3.50)¶

    Files: - Create: C:/Users/olive/SpritesWork/brain/scripts/sim_seeds_s101.py - Modify: C:/Users/olive/SpritesWork/brain/scripts/mirofish_sim_v2.py (add import)

    Sim definitions (50 agents x 10 steps x 3 rounds each): - Sim 101: Blind Discovery — all neutral, no priming, discover taxonomy from scratch - Sim 102: Taxonomy Audit — mix supportive/hostile, stress-test Tone/Structure/Precision - Sim 103: Pattern Detection Red Team — majority hostile, includes actual algorithm code

    • Step 1: Create sim_seeds_s101.py with 3 sim defs (101, 102, 103)
    • Step 2: Add import to mirofish_sim_v2.py
    • Step 3: Dry-run: python mirofish_sim_v2.py --sim 101 --dry-run
    • Step 4: Run all 3 in parallel (~15 min total)

    Task 7: BG-1 — Open Source Scenario Research¶

    Output: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-10-open-source-risk-analysis.md

    Research 7 scenarios with real precedents (Redis, Elastic, MongoDB, Sentry, Supabase, PostHog, io.js). Timeline at 1/6/12/24 months. Decision matrix: what to open vs keep proprietary.

    Task 8: MAIN — Supabase Migration¶

    • Step 1: Read cloud/migrations/001_initial_schema.sql
    • Step 2: Apply via Supabase MCP (project ref: miqwilxheuxwafvmoajs)
    • Step 3: Verify tables with SQL query

    WAVE 2 — After MiroFish Completes¶

    Task 9: Synthesize MiroFish + Optimize Weights¶

    • Step 1: Read 3 sim outputs
    • Step 2: Extract consensus: missing categories, gaps, recommended formula changes
    • Step 3: Apply structural changes to src/gradata/_manifest_quality.py
    • Step 4: Weight optimization: hill-climb on 29-brain stress test data (100 iterations, pure math)
    • Step 5: Test + commit

    WAVE 3 — After Wave 2¶

    Task 10: Ablation v2 — Improved System (budget ~$10)¶

    • Step 1: Re-run ablation with improved formula: --output-dir stress_test_results/ablation_v2/
    • Step 2: Compare baseline vs improved (win rates, deltas, per-domain)
    • Step 3: Write final comparison analysis
